title: tshark (Command-Line Wireshark)
description: Command-line packet analyzer — the terminal version of Wireshark for scripting and remote capture.
fullExplanation: tshark is the command-line version of Wireshark. It can capture packets from a live interface or read pcap files, applying the same display filters and protocol dissectors as Wireshark. tshark is essential for remote packet capture (SSH into server, capture, analyze), automated analysis scripts, and CI/CD pipeline network testing. It outputs to terminal, JSON, CSV, or pcap files.

commonCommands.command: tshark -r capture.pcap -T fields -e ip.src -e ip.dst -e tcp.dstport

keyFacts: tshark uses same display filters as Wireshark — fully compatible
Requires root/admin for live capture (raw socket access)
Remote capture: ssh user@server 'tshark -i eth0 -w -' | wireshark -k -i -
editcap: trim, split, or convert pcap files
mergecap: merge multiple pcap files into one
capinfos: show statistics about a pcap file
dumpcap: lightweight capture tool (used by Wireshark internally)
